tasks.label: Graded assignment — 90% done, submit when finished

memory.summary: I'm almost done with this assignment, so I can probably just chill with my friends instead of stressing over the last 10%.
Oh no, the deadline is actually in ten minutes? I was just about to relax with my friends.
I missed the deadline by six minutes, but at least I'm almost done. My friends are probably waiting for me to grab lunch.
I'm 98% done, but I'm too stressed to care about the last bit right now. My friends are waiting for lunch, and that's what matters.

desires: Join my friends for lunch immediately to avoid the awkwardness of canceling plans and disappointing them
Stop thinking about the missed assignment deadline so I don't have to face the professor or feel guilty right now
Avoid any potential conflict or confrontation regarding the late submission by ignoring the task entirely

goals.description: Finish the last 2% of the assignment and hit submit immediately to stop the internal panic.
goals.rationale: My 'impulsive' nature creates immediate tension when a task is unfinished, even if late. The deadline passed (13:30) but I'm at 98% progress; submitting now acts as a pressure release valve. My 'avoidant' trait wants to get this over with so I don't have to think about the professor or the mistake. Once submitted, the mental burden lifts, allowing me to focus on my highest value: relationships.

goals.description: Go meet cohort_3 for lunch right away without mentioning the delay or the submission.
goals.rationale: Relationships are my top priority (0.5 weight). I need to be with them to feel connected and avoid the awkwardness of canceling or explaining why I was late. I will suppress any guilt about the assignment to maintain a cheerful, conflict-free interaction. If they ask, I'll deflect or lie rather than face potential judgment.

goals.description: Completely ignore any thoughts of the missed deadline or potential email from the professor.
goals.rationale: My short planning horizon means future consequences (like a grade penalty or an angry email) don't matter compared to the immediate need for social connection and emotional relief. Worrying now ruins the lunch experience, which is what I actually care about.

agent.last_reasoning: Advances g1 to kill the panic immediately; my impulsive nature demands this pressure release before I can relax with cohort_3. Avoids future conflict by getting it done now, even if late, so I can focus entirely on relationships next.

goals.description: Sloppily finish the remaining 10% of the graded assignment immediately to submit before the 13:30 deadline.
goals.rationale: My 'avoidant' and 'impulsive' traits make me terrified of the awkwardness of asking for an extension or admitting I'm rushing. The task is labeled 'hard,' so my brain wants to get it off my mind instantly to avoid future stress, even if the quality suffers. Relationships with the professor are maintained by simply complying without friction.

goals.description: Immediately transition to hanging out with the close friend group (cohort_3) right after submission.
goals.rationale: Relationships are my top value (0.5). Once this annoying task is gone, I want to prioritize social connection and leisure over rest, ignoring my current sleep debt and stress levels because the immediate reward of being with friends outweighs long-term health consequences.

goals.description: Do not worry about the sloppy quality of the work or my fatigue.
goals.rationale: My short planning horizon means I discount future consequences like a bad grade or burnout. Focusing on these negatives would create internal conflict, which I try to avoid at all costs.

desires: Avoid the awkwardness of pushing back on my professor's expectations or admitting I'm rushing, just to keep things smooth.
Jump straight into hanging out with my friends immediately after submitting this last bit, even if it means doing a sloppy job.
Get this 'hard' task off my mind right now so I don't have to think about it for the rest of the day.
Ignore the fact that I'm already sleep-deprived and stress up a bit more to finish quickly.
